title: Locks should be released within the same method
description: This rule raises if you acquire a lock with one of the following methods, and do not release it within the same method. ReaderWriterLock.AcquireReaderLock ReaderWriterLock.AcquireWriterLock ReaderWriterLockSlim.EnterReadLock ReaderWriterLockSlim.EnterUpgradeableReadLock ReaderWriterLockSlim.TryEnterReadLock ReaderWriterLockSlim.TryEnterUpgradeableReadLock ReaderWriterLockSlim.EnterWriteLock ReaderWriterLockSlim.TryEnterWriteLock This rule will raise an issue when the code uses the disposable pattern. This pattern makes locking easy to use and delegates the responsibility to the caller.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.triggering_conditions: Lock acquisition method called without corresponding release method in same method body
exclusion_conditions: Both acquire and release methods present in same method
never_trigger_on: Methods where acquire and release are properly paired
logic.reasoning: Detects ReaderWriterLock and ReaderWriterLockSlim lock acquisitions (AcquireReaderLock, AcquireWriterLock, EnterReadLock, EnterWriteLock, etc.) that are not released within the same method. This violates best practices as callers become responsible for releasing locks, leading to potential errors and deadlocks.

should_trigger: public void AcquireWriterLock() => rwLock.AcquireWriterLock(2000); // No release in method
public void StartRead() { rwLockSlim.EnterReadLock(); } // No ExitReadLock
